response:
D

The planets revolve around the Sun due to gravitational attraction. The Sun contains the vast majority of the mass in the solar system (approximately 99.8%), which makes its gravitational pull by far the strongest. This force keeps all the planets in their orbits. 

Option A is incorrect because the Sun's composition (mostly hydrogen and helium) does not explain orbital motion. Option B is incorrect because age has no bearing on gravitational force. Option C is incorrect because while the Sun does provide heat and light, those forms of energy do not cause the planets to revolve in their orbits.
